---
title: "References"
---

# References and further reading {.unnumbered}

## Source code

All five fuel-upload engines live as sibling directories in this repository:

- `normalcsharp-fuel-engine/` — the deliberately junior-to-mid C# baseline this book is written against.
- `csharp-fuel-engine/` — idiomatic C# with sealed-record DUs, NRT, switch expressions, and a V3 boundary (CSV import, repository ports, audit, operational report).
- `fsharp-fuel-engine/` — F# with native discriminated unions, exhaustive match, and a V3 `Interop.fs` boundary plus `Audit.fs` and `OperationalBatchReport.fs`.
- `haskell-fuel-engine/` — Haskell with algebraic data types, `NonEmpty`, `Either`-based errors, and QuickCheck property tests.
- `rust-fuel-engine/` — Rust with algebraic enums, exhaustive `match`, and `TryFrom`-style boundary conversion.

Build and run instructions live in [Setup](setup.qmd).

## Experiment docs (in `docs/`)

- `docs/v2-experiment-plan.md` — the V2 round (idiomatic refactors, quarantined outcome, split recovery modes, DTO mapping).
- `docs/v2-results.md` — V2 cross-language scoring.
- `docs/v2-scoring-rubric.md` — what V2 graded on.
- `docs/v3-experiment-plan.md` — the V3 round (integration pressure: CSV, repositories, audit, operational report).
- `docs/v3-phase-briefing.md` — V3 phase-by-phase diagrams and rationale.
- `docs/v3-results.md` — V3 cross-language scoring and verdicts.
- `docs/v3-scoring-rubric.md` — what V3 graded on.
- `docs/fsharp-learning-guide.md` — companion learning guide for the F# implementation (the V3 reference).
- `docs/PROPERTY-TESTING.md` — notes on the QuickCheck / FsCheck setup.
- `docs/agent-notes/` — per-phase agent decision logs for both V2 and V3.

## External reading

- Paul Graham, ["Beating the Averages"](http://www.paulgraham.com/avg.html) — the original blub-paradox essay.
- Scott Wlaschin, *Domain Modeling Made Functional* — the canonical "design with types" book; the F# decision-engine in this repo is directly informed by it.
- Matt Parsons, ["Type Safety Back and Forth"](https://www.parsonsmatt.org/2017/10/11/type_safety_back_and_forth.html) — about pushing partial functions out of the domain core.
- Yaron Minsky, ["Effective ML"](https://blog.janestreet.com/effective-ml-revisited/) — "make illegal states unrepresentable" in OCaml; the same lesson applies to F# and Haskell.
- Alexis King, ["Parse, Don't Validate"](https://lexi-lambda.github.io/blog/2019/11/05/parse-don-t-validate/) — the boundary discipline that V3 stress-tests in this book.
- *The Rust Programming Language* (the official book) — chapter 6 on enums and pattern matching maps directly onto the Rust engine's `RowDecision`.
- Haskell *Real World Haskell* and *Haskell Programming from First Principles* — for the type-system primer this book skips.

## How this book was built

- Authored in [Quarto](https://quarto.org) using fenced Markdown plus Mermaid diagrams.
- Source on GitHub: <https://github.com/tedk99/experiment-normalcsharp>.
- Each chapter quotes real source verbatim from the five engines; nothing is invented.
- The book is intentionally short and re-readable. If something is unclear, the engine source is the ground truth — open the file path in the snippet caption and read around it.
